info.name: Factory+
info.description:
Factory+ HTTP API collection for the AMRC Connectivity Stack.

**Authentication.** All requests inherit OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code with PKCE) against the cluster's Keycloak `acs-cli` public client. Click the collection's Authorization tab → Get New Access Token to obtain a JWT, then Use Token. Pings explicitly opt out via per-request `noauth`.

**Setup.** Set the `scheme` (http/https) and `domain` (the ACS base hostname) collection variables to your deployment. Every request URL derives from those plus the per-service subdomain - no other configuration needed.

**Coverage.** All HTTP-facing F+ services on the cluster: auth, configdb, directory, cmdesc, files, git, cluster-manager (clusters subdomain), i3x. Services not listed here (monitor, historians, edge agents, etc.) speak only Sparkplug/MQTT and have no HTTP surface to test.

**Requires** the JWT auth feature deployed (v5.2.0-i3x.6 or newer). Older ACS releases reject Keycloak JWTs and need Basic/Negotiate auth instead.

variable.description: http for local/dev clusters, https for production
ACS deployment base hostname (matches acs.baseUrl in the Helm chart)

item.name: info

request.description: i3x's public /v1/info endpoint. Returns spec version + capability flags.

item.description: Public health checks. No auth required - useful for smoke-testing reachability before doing anything else.

item.name: auth

request.description: Returns the full identity record for the calling principal. With a Keycloak JWT, fp_principal_uuid is used to look up the record.
Recursive group membership for a principal (used by the SPI for the fp_groups claim).
Identity record of a given kind (kerberos, sparkplug, etc.) for a principal.

item.name: legacy v1
item.description: Kept for the service-client + older callers. New code should use /v2.

request.description: Bulk-load a dump file. Restricted to privileged callers.
Mint an opaque F+ session token. Authenticate with Basic/Negotiate; the token then represents your identity to THIS service only. Not needed for JWT callers - the JWT itself acts as the token.

item.description:
Factory+ auth service.

/v2/* is the modern API and should be preferred. /authz/* and /authn/* are legacy compat surfaces kept for older clients (notably the service-client library before it was updated). /load is dump loading. /token mints opaque session tokens (not needed when using JWTs).

item.name: configdb

request.description: Members of Class.App. Compat shim that forwards to /v2/class/<App>/member.

item.name: v1 (legacy / search)
item.description: Compatibility surface that mostly forwards to v2. Search lives only here.
Configuration store. /v2 is the modern API; /v1 is a compatibility forwarder for legacy clients. Prefer /v2.

item.name: directory
item.description: Sparkplug device/node registry. Lists running devices, their history, service advertisements, alerts, schemas, and inter-object links.

item.name: cmdesc
item.description: Command Escalation. POST a Sparkplug command (write metric) to an edge node or device by address. The body is the metric to write.

item.name: files

request.description: Upload as a new file - server assigns a UUID.

item.description: Blob store. Each file is keyed by UUID. ACL is enforced per-file.

item.name: git

request.description: Remove a repo's on-disk storage (does not delete the ConfigDB entry).

item.description:
Git repository hosting (git-http-backend over HTTP) plus storage management.

The /git/:group/:repo/* and /uuid/:uuid/* paths serve the git smart HTTP protocol (clone/push/fetch) - they aren't useful in Postman, use `git` itself. The /v1 endpoints are for storage management.

item.name: cluster-manager

request.description: Returns the bootstrap manifest to apply to a new edge cluster.

item.description: Edge Cluster Manager. Cluster provisioning, bootstrap manifests, sealed secret management for edge clusters.

item.name: i3x
subscriptions
item.description: Live value subscriptions. Stream via SSE or poll via sync.

i3X v1 REST/SSE API. Northbound surface for third-party REST clients - this is the primary consumer of the new JWT auth flow.

/v1/info is public; all other endpoints require a JWT.
